name: Arabic alphabet
description: The 28 letters of the Arabic abjad, shown in isolated form (letters connect and change shape by position). Written right-to-left. Each letter is tested both ways, tracked separately.
primer:
The Arabic alphabet is an **abjad** of **28 letters** written **right-to-left**. It marks consonants and long vowels; short vowels are added as optional diacritics. Most letters change shape depending on position (isolated / initial / medial / final) and connect to their neighbours — but several letters never join to the *following* letter (ا د ذ ر ز و). The forms below are the **isolated** forms. Several letters are distinguished only by dots above or below the same base shape (e.g. ب/ت/ث, ج/ح/خ). Arabic also has *emphatic* (pharyngealised) consonants written with ṣ/ḍ/ṭ/ẓ.

**Full mapping — letter → name → sound:**

- **ا** — alif — long 'a' / glottal stop
- **ب** — ba — b
- **ت** — ta — t
- **ث** — tha — th (as in 'think')
- **ج** — jim — j
- **ح** — ha — emphatic h (ḥ)
- **خ** — kha — kh (as in 'loch')
- **د** — dal — d
- **ذ** — dhal — dh (as in 'this')
- **ر** — ra — r (rolled)
- **ز** — zay — z
- **س** — sin — s
- **ش** — shin — sh
- **ص** — sad — emphatic s (ṣ)
- **ض** — dad — emphatic d (ḍ) — Arabic is nicknamed 'the language of the ḍād'
- **ط** — ta — emphatic t (ṭ)
- **ظ** — za — emphatic dh/z (ẓ)
- **ع** — ayn — voiced pharyngeal (ʿ)
- **غ** — ghayn — gh (guttural)
- **ف** — fa — f
- **ق** — qaf — q (uvular k)
- **ك** — kaf — k
- **ل** — lam — l
- **م** — mim — m
- **ن** — nun — n
- **ه** — ha — h
- **و** — waw — w (or long 'u')
- **ي** — ya — y (or long 'i')

**Special cases:** ا و ي double as the long vowels ā / ū / ī; ع (ayn) and ء (hamza, the glottal stop) have no English equivalent; the emphatic pairs (ص/س, ض/د, ط/ت, ظ/ذ) sound deeper/back in the throat than their plain counterparts.

cards.note: Arabic is nicknamed 'the language of the ḍād'.
